title: Fix CSS Variable Priority with Nested Selectors and Dynamic Grid Layout

statement.markdown: You are given a CSS file with a dynamic grid layout using CSS variables. The grid has a variable number of rows and columns, and the CSS variables are used to define the styles for each row and column. However, there is a bug in the CSS code that causes the variable priority to be inconsistent when using nested selectors. Fix the bug by making sure that the variable priority is consistent across all selectors.
statement.inputFormat: CSS file content as a string
statement.outputFormat: Valid CSS file content with consistent variable priority
statement.notes: The CSS file should be parsed and the bug should be fixed without modifying the existing styles. The input CSS file may contain multiple nested selectors and CSS variables.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the grid
Number of columns in the grid
Number of CSS variables used in the file

examples.explanation: The bug is fixed by adding a new nested selector that uses the variable --k to specify the nth-child index of the grid-item.

hints.text: Check the CSS variable priority in the nested selectors

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Variable Priority with Nested Selectors and Dynamic Grid Layout
editorial.intuition: The bug can be fixed by adding a new nested selector that uses the variable --k to specify the nth-child index of the grid-item.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to manually add a new nested selector for each combination of nth-child indices.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use a CSS preprocessor like Sass or Less to generate the nested selectors dynamically.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a CSS preprocessor like Sass or Less to generate the nested selectors dynamically, and then use a CSS parser to validate the output CSS file.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the optimal solution produces the correct output CSS file for any input CSS file.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to illustrate the solution
